WITH (SELECT toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.999999999', 9)) AS dt64 SELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(dt64);
WITH (SELECT toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.999999999', 9)) AS dt64 SELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(dt64, 'Europe/London');
WITH (SELECT toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.123456789', 9)) AS dt64 SELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(dt64) AS rounded_time, dt64;
SELECT t1.event_time, toStartOfMicrosecond(t1.event_time) FROM (SELECT event_time FROM timezone_test) AS t1;
SELECT event_time, COUNT(*) FROM timezone_test GROUP BY toStartOfMicrosecond(event_time) ORDER BY event_time;
WITH (SELECT toDateTime64('2020-01-01 10:20:30.999999999', 9)) AS dt64 SELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(dt64), COUNT(*) FROM timezone_test GROUP BY toStartOfMicrosecond(event_time) HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
